creator: ports-x11-manpages-may07@oldach.net
text:
X.org has some 700+ manpages that just contain a ".so <somethingelse>"
line. This breaks our catman scheme with compressed manpages, resulting
in over 700 unusable manpages. This currently applies to the following
X.org-related ports:

x11-fonts/libXfontcache
x11-toolkits/libXt
x11/bitmap
x11/libX11
x11/libXScrnSaver
x11/libXau
x11/libXevie
x11/libXext
x11/libXi
x11/libXrandr
x11/libXres
x11/libXxf86dga
x11/libXxf86misc
x11/libXxf86vm
x11/xtrap

We should use our standard MLINKS mechanism instead. The patch below
fixes it.
